Article: The new NERV Bases in Rebuild
[]: The new NERV Bases in Rebuild             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Mar 20, 2011 4:06 pm I've just stumbled over something that may or may not be important - You all know Mari, and you also know that her first battle takes place at a place called Bethany Base. Now, some of you maybe already knew that, but there is a biblical person called Mary of Bethany ...A Coincidence? I think not. Of course, there's always the possibility that they thought Mari up, but lacked a Name for the Base, ans since all the new ones from Rebuild are named after biblical places, Anno simply searched Wikipedia for a biblical city with some connection to some person called Mary and thought "Oh, Bethania sounds cool!" Or he thought "Oh, Time for some gratitious biblic references!" Named bethany base and then looked for other cool sounding Names, as the other names don't show that blatant connections to the plot. Which still raises the question whether Mari's Name is meant to be the Japanese "Mari" (=Ball, truth, jasmine, long distance, depending on the Kanji) or indeed the English/Hebrew Mary, or "Mari" as in the Welsh form of "Mary" (The latter two are possible, since she's british and we don't know what part of Britain she's from) One might also wonder if there is a Martha Makinami, tough probably not, the "Martha" character here is Asuka, Enokido discribed Mary as "Not unearthly like Rei, but not practical like Asuka either, like a Priestess chanelling the gods". That "Mary of Bethania" was listening to Jesus' teachings instead of helping her sister with work - and indeed, Mari has to this Day never appeared in a domestic setting, for most her screentime, she is busy with "the divine" (=The EVAs, the prophecy...) Maybe this implies something about what her relationship with Shinji (= the Messiah of sorts, even if he isn't really acting like one) or Kaworu (Who acts way more messiaic). Or, to cross the line into fanwank completely, she might be Asuka's sister, explaining the Unit 02 piloting bit! XD Forget the last one. Seriously. Well, to move on to the other new Base names: Tabgha (Moon Base). It means "Seven Springs" (It's the

Article: The new NERV Bases in Rebuild
uka's sister, explaining the Unit 02 piloting bit! XD Forget the last one. Seriously. Well, to move on to the other new Base names: Tabgha (Moon Base). It means "Seven Springs" (It's the Seventh Branch!) and it was also called "The Solitude" (It's on the moon, an isolated, lonely place surrounded by nothingness. It is also the place where the multiplying of the fish and bread took place - Are they perhaps building the MP EVAs along with Mark 6? It is also the fourth place where Jesus appeared after his ressurrection (Oh, FOUR! Anything associated with four MUST be bad in NGE - and it is, it's where SEELE builds their stuff. Seven, of course, refers to the SEELE logo) And now, on to Golgotha (Where the Dummy Plug was made): The place where the messiah was crucified ... perhaps tied to Shinji suffering because of the Dummyplugs? It means "place of skulls" or "hill of execution" It's the new Base we know the least about, it might become meaningful later, as the trailer featured Chibi Reis... All we know is that the Dummy Plugs come from there and that even Kaji doesn't have any information on that place.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Mar 20, 2011 6:21 pm ...

I guess... (The Biblical name "Mary" is " Mari a" in Japanese, BTW.)
